name: ACQ_CERT
label: Acquiring FDIC Certificate #
definition: Acquiring Institution's unique number assigned by the FDIC used to identify institutions and for the issuance of insurance certificates.

name: ACQ_CHANGECODE
label: Acquiring Activity Event Code
definition: Acquiring Institution Activity Event Code

name: ACQ_CHARTAGENT
label: Acquiring Chartering Agency
definition: Acquiring Institution Chartering Agency - State and Federal, Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: ACQ_CHARTER
label: Acquiring OCC Charter Number
definition: Acquiring Institution's unique number assigned by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) used to identify institutions it has chartered and regulates (i.e. national banks).

name: ACQ_CLASS
label: Acquiring Class Designation
definition: Acquiring Institution's class designation code indicating the major groupings of institutions. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: ACQ_CLCODE
label: Acquiring Numeric Class Code
definition: Acquiring Institution's numeric code which identifies the major and minor categories of an institution.

name: ACQ_CNTYNAME
label: Acquiring County Name
definition: County where the acquiring institution is physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: ACQ_CNTYNUM
label: Acquiring County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code of the acquiring institution.

name: ACQ_FDICREGION
label: Acquiring Supervisory Region Number
definition: A two-digit number indicating the FDIC Supervisory Division or Region of the acquiring institution. 02 = New York; 05 = Atlanta; 09 = Chicago; 11 = Kansas City; 13 = Dallas; 14 = San Francisco; 16 = Office of Complex Financial Institutions (CFI)

name: ACQ_FDICREGION_DESC
label: Acquiring Supervisory Region Description
definition: The description of the FDIC supervisory region of the acquiring institution.

name: ACQ_INSAGENT1
label: Acquiring Insurance Fund Membership
definition: The insurance fund membership of the acquiring institution; Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF), Bank Insurance Fund (BIF), Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: ACQ_INSAGENT2
label: Acquiring Secondary Insurance Fund
definition: The secondary insurance fund of the acquiring institution. As a result of the establishment of a single Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF) effective April 1, 2006, the secondary insurance fund is no longer applicable. Prior to April 1, 2006 secondary funds included both Bank Insurance Fund (BIF) and Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: ACQ_INSTNAME
label: Acquiring Institution Name
definition: Name of the Acquiring Institution

name: ACQ_MADDR
label: Acquiring Mailing Street Address
definition: Street address of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MADDR2
label: Acquiring Mailing Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MCITY
label: Acquiring Mailing City
definition: City of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MSTALP
label: Acquiring Mailing State Abbreviation
definition: State abbreviation of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MSTATE
label: Acquiring Mailing State
definition: State of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MZIP5
label: Acquiring Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing physical location of the institution or its branch office.

name: ACQ_MZIP5_RAW
label: Acquiring 5-digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code of the acquiring institution's mailing address.

name: ACQ_MZIPREST
label: Acquiring Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension of the acquiring institution's mailing address.

name: ACQ_ORG_EFF_DTE
label: Acquiring Instituion's Effective Date
definition: Acquiring Institution's Effective Date

name: ACQ_PADDR
label: Acquiring Physical Street Address
definition: Street address at which the acquiring institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: ACQ_PADDR2
label: Acquiring Physical Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 at which the acquiring institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: ACQ_PCITY
label: Acquiring Physical City
definition: City in which an acquiring institution's main office or one of its branches is physically located.

name: ACQ_PSTALP
label: Acquiring Physical State Alpha Code
definition: State in which the acquiring institution's main office or one if its branches are physically located. The FDIC Act defines state as any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Virgin Island, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

name: ACQ_PZIP5
label: Acquiring Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing physical location of the acquiring institution or one of its branch offices.

name: ACQ_PZIP5_RAW
label: Acquiring Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing physical location of the institution or its branch office.

name: ACQ_PZIPREST
label: Acquiring Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension of the acquiring institution's physical location.

name: ACQ_REGAGENT
label: Acquiring Chartering Agency
definition: Acquiring Institution Chartering Agency - State and Federal, Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: ACQ_TRUST
label: Acquiring Trust Power
definition: Trust power status of the acquiring institution.

name: ACQ_UNINUM
label: Acquiring Unique FDIC Number
definition: The acquiring institution's unique FDIC identifier number for holding companies, banks, branches and nondeposit subsidiaries. This value maps to the main office for the acquiring Institution in a merger, acquisition, etc.

name: ACTIVE
label: Institution Status
definition: A number indicating the status of an institution. 1='Institutions that are currently open and insured by the FDIC'; 0='Institution closed or not insured by FDIC'

name: ACT_EVT1_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT2_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT3_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT4_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT5_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT_DESC
label: Activity Event Description
definition: The description of a code that identifies an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number indicating a change/event to an institution's activities or ownership over time.

name: ADDRESBR
label: ADDRESBR
definition: Physical location of the branch

name: ADDRESS
label: Street Address
definition: The street address in which an institution or branch office is physically located.

name: ADDRESS2
label: Street Address Line 2
definition: The street address line 2 in which an institution or branch office is physically located.

name: ASSET
label: ASSET
definition: Total assets of the institution as of June 30.   This amount is repeated for every location of the institution.  Be sure to use the assets only once for each institution, otherwise duplication of the assets when summarizing by institution.  Please note that assets are not available by location.  They are only reported at the institution level on the quarterly Call Reports and are assigned to the headquarter's location.

name: BKCLASS
label: Institution Class
definition: A classification code assigned by the FDIC based on the institution's charter type (commercial bank or savings institution), charter agent (state or federal), Federal Reserve membership status (Fed member, Fed non-member) and its primary federal regulator (state chartered institutions are subject to both federal and state supervision). N - Commercial bank, national (federal) charter, Fed member, and supervised by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC); NM - Commercial bank, state charter, Fed non-member, and supervised by the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC); OI - Insured U.S. branch of a foreign chartered institution (IBA) and supervised by the OCC or FDIC; SB Federal savings banks, federal charter, supervised by the OCC or before July 21,2011 the Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS); SI - State chartered stock savings banks, supervised by the FDIC; SL - State chartered stock savings and loan associations, supervised by the FDIC or before July 21,2011 the OTS; SM - Commercial bank, state charter, Fed member, and supervised by the Federal Reserve Bank (FRB); NC Noninsured non-deposit commercial banks and/or trust companies regulated by the OCC, a state, or a territory; NS - Noninsured stock savings bank supervised by a state or territory; CU - state or federally chartered credit unions supervised by the National Credit Union Association (NCUA).

name: BKMO
label: BKMO
definition: Main office / branch identifier; Main office  = 1, branch = 0

name: BRCENM
label: BRCENM
definition: Code used to describe the nature of the reported deposits: C - Combined E - Estimated N - Non Deposit M - Main Office

name: BRNUM
label: BRNUM
definition: Numerical reference to identify a branch office within one institution

name: BRSERTYP
label: BRSERTYP
definition: Code that defines the type of service the branch office provides:                                                               11- Full Service, brick and mortar office 12- Full Service, retail office 13- Full Service, cyber office 21- Limited Service, administrative office 22- Limited Service, military facility 23- Limited Service, drive-through facility 24- Limited Service, loan production office 25- Limited Service, consumer credit office 26- Limited Service, contractual office 27- Limited Service, messenger office 28- Limited Service, retail office 29- Limited Service, mobile/seasonal office 30- Limited Service, trust office

name: CALL
label: CALL
definition: Prior to 2012, OTS institutions filed a quarterly Thrift Financial Report (TFR). All other institutions file the CALL report.

name: CB
label: Community Bank
definition: A flag used to indicate whether an institution is a community bank. 0 = Not a community Bank. 1 = Is a community bank. For more information, visit the community bank research program page: https://www.fdic.gov/community-banking-research-program.

name: CBSA
label: Core Based Statistical Area Name
definition: Name of the Core Based Statistical Area (CBSA) as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget.

name: CBSA_DIV
label: Metropolitan Divisions Name
definition: Name of the Core Based Statistical Division as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget.

name: CBSA_DIV_FLG
label: Metropolitan Divisions Flag
definition: A flag (1=Yes) indicating member of a Core Based Statistical Division as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget.

name: CBSA_DIV_NAMB
label: CBSA_DIV_NAMB
definition: The name that corresponds to a code for a county or group of counties within a Core Based Statistical Area that contains a core with a population of at least 2.5 million. A Metropolitan Division consists of one or more main/secondary counties that represent an employment center or centers, plus adjacent counties associated with the main county or counties through commuting ties.

name: CBSA_DIV_NO
label: Metropolitan Divisions Number
definition: Numeric code of the Core Based Statistical Division as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget.

name: CBSA_METRO
label: Metropolitan Division Number
definition: Numeric code of the Metropolitan Statistical Area as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CBSA_METRO_FLG
label: Metropolitan Division Flag
definition: A flag (1=Yes) used to indicate whether an branch is in a Metropolitan Statistical Area as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CBSA_METRO_NAME
label: Metropolitan Division Name
definition: Name of the Metropolitan Statistical Area as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CBSA_MICRO_FLG
label: Micropolitan Division Flag
definition: A flag (1=Yes) used to indicate whether an branch is in a Metropolitan Statistical Area as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CBSA_NO
label: Core Based Statistical Areas
definition: Numeric code of the Core Based Statistical Area (CBSA) as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget.

name: CERT
label: FDIC Certificate #
definition: A unique number assigned by the FDIC used to identify institutions and for the issuance of insurance certificates.

name: CERTCONS
label: Directly owned by another bank (CERT)
definition: FDIC certificate number of the parent bank or savings institution with which the reported institution s financial data has been consolidated. Beginning in March 1997, both the Thrift Financial Reports and Call Reports are completed on a fully consolidated basis. Previously, the consolidation of subsidiary depository institutions was prohibited. Now, parent institutions are required to file consolidated reports, while their subsidiary financial institutions are still required to file separate reports. Click on the certificate number to identify the parent bank or thrift.

name: CFPBEFFDTE
label: CFPB Effective Date
definition: Date the institution began secondary supervision by CFPB

name: CFPBENDDTE
label: CFPB End Date
definition: Date the institution ended supervision by CFPB

name: CFPBFLAG
label: CFPB Flag
definition: Indicates secondary supervision by CFPB ('0' - not supervised by CFPB, '1'- secondarily supervised by CFPB)

name: CHANGEC1
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC10
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC11
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC12
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC13
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC14
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC15
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC2
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC3
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC4
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC5
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC6
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC7
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC8
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGEC9
label: Change Code
definition: FDIC code used to signify a structural event relating to an institution.

name: CHANGECODE
label: Activity Event Code
definition: Activity Event Code

name: CHANGECODE_DESC
label: Activity Event Code Description
definition: Activity Event Code Description

name: CHARTAGENT
label: Chartering Agency
definition: The name of the chartering agency of the institution.  OCC = Comptroller Of The Currency OTS = Office Of Thrift Supervision State = State Agency Sover = Foreign Country

name: CHARTER
label: OCC Charter Number
definition: The charter number identifying a financial institution supervised by the Office of Comptroller of Currency.

name: CHRTAGNN
label: CHRTAGNN
definition: The name of the chartering agency of the institution. OCC   = COMPTROLLER OF THE CURRENCY OTS   = OFFICE OF THRIFT SUPERVISION STATE = STATE AGENCY SOVER = FOREIGN COUNTRY

name: CHRTAGNT
label: Chartering Agency
definition: All Chartering Agencies - State and Federal Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: CITY
label: City
definition: The city in which an institution or branch office is physically located.

name: CITY2BR
label: CITY2BR
definition: Central city based on the reported ZIP code of the branch

name: CITYBR
label: CITYBR
definition: Reported city in which the branch is located

name: CITYHCR
label: City of High Holder
definition: City in which the headquarters of the institution's regulatory high holder are physically located.

name: CLASS
label: Bank Charter Class
definition: A code indicating the major groupings of institutions. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: CLCODE
label: Numeric Code
definition: A two-digit identifying category of an institution. 03 = National bank, Federal Reserve System (FRS) member; 13 = State commercial bank, FRS member; 15 = State industrial bank, FRS member; 21 = State commercial bank, not FRS member; 23 = State industrial bank, not FRS member; 25 - State nonmember mutual bank; 33 = Federal chartered savings and co-operative bank - stock; 34 = Federal chartered savings and co-operative bank - mutual; 43 = Federal chartered stock savings bank (historical); 44 = Federal chartered mutual savings bank (historical); 35 = State chartered thrift - stock; 36 = State chartered thrift - mutual; 37 = Federal chartered thrift - stock; 38 = Federal chartered thrift - mutual; 41 = State chartered stock savings and co-operative bank; 42 = State chartered mutual savings and co-operative bank; 52 = Insured domestic offices of foreign banks (International Banking Act(IBA)); 50 = Nondeposit trust company, OCC chartered; 51 = Commercial bank; 52 = Noninsured domestic offices of foreign bank (IBA); 53 = Industrial bank; 54 = Nondeposit trust company, state chartered, not FRS member; 57 = New York investment company; 58 = Nondeposit trust company, state chartered, FRS member; 59 = Nondeposit trust company; 61 = Noninsured private bank; 62 = Noninsured loan workout bank, OCC chartered; 63 = Noninsured loan workout bank, state chartered, FRS member; 64 = Noninsured loan workout bank, state chartered, not FRS member; 81 = Noninsured stock savings and co-operative bank; 82 = Noninsured mutual savings and co-operative bank; 85 = Noninsured stock savings and loan association; 86 = Noninsured mutual savings and loan association; 89 = Noninsured insurance company.

name: CNTRYNA
label: CNTRYNA
definition: The country name where the headquarters of the institution is chartered.

name: CNTRYNAB
label: CNTRYNAB
definition: Country name in which the branch is located.

name: CNTYNAMB
label: CNTYNAMB
definition: County name in which the branch is located.

name: CNTYNAME
label: County
definition: The county name in which an institution or branch office is physically located.

name: CNTYNUM
label: County Number
definition: The Federal Information Processing System (FIPS) number corresponding to the county in which a physical address is located.

name: CNTYNUMB
label: CNTYNUMB
definition: County number that corresponds to the county in which the branch is located.

name: CONSERVE
label: Conservatorship
definition: A flag (Y=yes;N=no) that indicates if an institution is being operated in government conservatorship.

name: CONSOLD
label: CONSOLD
definition: When BRCENM = C, consolidated, this is the office number (brnum) in which the deposits are reported.  Both branches should be within the same state and county.

name: COUNTY
label: County
definition: The county name in which an institution or branch office is physically located.

name: CSA
label: Combined Statistical Area Name (Branch)
definition: Name of the Combined Statistical Area (CSA) as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CSABR
label: CSABR
definition: The identifying code of a geographic entity consisting of two or more adjacent Core Based Statistical Areas (CBSAs) with employment interchange measures of at least 15. Pairs of CBSAs with employment interchange measures of at least 25 combine automatically. Pairs of CBSAs with employment interchange measures of at least 15, but less than 25, may combine if local opinion in both areas favors combination.

name: CSANAMBR
label: CSANAMBR
definition: The name of a geographic entity consisting of two or more adjacent Core Based Statistical Areas (CBSAs) with employment interchange measures of at least 15. Pairs of CBSAs with employment interchange measures of at least 25 combine automatically. Pairs of CBSAs with employment interchange measures of at least 15, but less than 25, may combine if local opinion in both areas favors combination.

name: CSA_FLG
label: Combined Statistical Area Flag  (Branch)
definition: Flag (1=Yes) indicating member of a Combined Statistical Area (CSA) as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CSA_NO
label: Combined Statistical Area Number (Branch)
definition: Numeric code of the Combined Statistical Area (CSA) as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: DATEUPDT
label: Last update
definition: The date of the last data update.

name: DENOVO
label: Denovo Institution
definition: A flag used to indicate whether an institution is a new institution (not a recharter). This flag is set quarterly. For instance, if REPDTE is 3/31/98 and DENOVO equals 1, the institution was a denovo during the first quarter of 1998.

name: DEP
label: Total deposits
definition: The sum of all deposits including demand deposits, money market deposits, other savings deposits, time deposits and deposits in foreign offices.

name: DEPDOM
label: Deposits held in domestic offices
definition: The sum of all domestic office deposits, including demand deposits, money market deposits, other savings deposits and time deposits.

name: DEPSUM
label: DEPSUM
definition: Total deposits of the institution as of June 30.

name: DEPSUMBR
label: DEPSUMBR
definition: Branch office deposits as of June 30

name: DIVISIONB
label: DIVISIONB
definition: A county or group of counties within a Core Based Statistical Area that contains a core with a population of at least 2.5 million. A Metropolitan Division consists of one or more main/secondary counties that represent an employment center or centers, plus adjacent counties associated with the main county or counties through commuting ties.

name: DOCKET
label: OTS Docket Number
definition: An identification number assigned to institutions chartered by the Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) or members of the Federal Housing Finance Board (FHFB) and formerly by the Federal Home Loan Bank (FHLB) Board. The value is '00000' for institutions not members of the FHFB.

name: EFFDATE
label: Last Structure Change Effective Date
definition: A date indicating when an institution's change/event is effective.

name: ENDDATE
label: End Effective Date
definition: End Effective Date

name: ENDEFYMD
label: End date
definition: The date that ends or closes out the last structural event relating to an institution. For closed institutions, this date represents the day that the institution became inactive.

name: EQ
label: Equity capital
definition: Total equity capital (includes preferred and common stock, surplus and undivided profits).

name: ESCROW
label: ESCROW
definition: Escrow deposits report on the Thrift Financial Reports.  These are included in the total domestic deposits on the Call Reports.

name: ESTYMD
label: Established Date
definition: The date on which the institution or branch began operations.

name: FDICDBS
label: FDIC Geographic Region
definition: The FDIC Office assigned to the geographic area. The eight FDIC Regions and their respective states are: Boston - Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, Vermont New York - Delaware, District of Columbia, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, U.S. Virgin Islands Atlanta - Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, West Virginia Memphis - Arkansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Mississippi, Tennessee Chicago - Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin Kansas City - Iowa, Kansas, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, South Dakota Dallas - Colorado, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Texas San Francisco - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, States of Micronesia, Utah, Washington, Wyoming

name: FDICNAME
label: FDICNAME
definition: The name of the FDIC Regional Office that services the institution.

name: FDICREGION
label: Supervisory Region
definition: A two-digit number indicating the FDIC Supervisory Division or Region. 02 = New York; 05 = Atlanta; 09 = Chicago; 11 = Kansas City; 13 = Dallas; 14 = San Francisco; 16 = Office of Complex Financial Institutions (CFI)

name: FDICREGION_DESC
label: Supervisory Region Description
definition: A description associated with the name of an FDIC supervisory region

name: FDICREGN
label: FDIC Supervisory Region
definition: The supervisory FDIC office assigned to the institution. The eight FDIC Supervisory Regions and their respective states are: Boston - Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, Vermont New York - Delaware, District of Columbia, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, U.S. Virgin Islands Atlanta - Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, West Virginia Memphis - Arkansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Mississippi, Tennessee Chicago - Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin Kansas City - Iowa, Kansas, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, South Dakota Dallas - Colorado, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Texas San Francisco - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, States of Micronesia, Utah, Washington, Wyoming

name: FDICSUPV
label: Federal Reserve District
definition: The supervisory FDIC office assigned to the institution. There are seven Federal Reserve Districts, with two Districts serving one state in some instances. The list of Federal Reserve Districts and their respective states are as follows: New York - Connecticut, Delaware, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, Rhode Island, U.S. Virgin Islands, Vermont, Atlanta - Alabama, Arkansas, Florida, Georgia, Louisiana, Mississippi, North Dakota, South Dakota, Tennessee , Chicago - Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin, Kansas City - Colorado, Kansas, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, New Mexico, North Dakota, Oklahoma, South Dakota, Wyoming , Dallas - Arkansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, New Mexico, Texas, San Francisco - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, States of Micronesia, Utah, Washington, Washington - Delaware, Maryland, North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, West Virginia

name: FED
label: Federal Reserve ID Number
definition: A number used to identify the Federal Reserve district in which the institution is located. 1 - Boston, 2 - New York, 3 - Philadelphia, 4 - Cleveland, 5 - Richmond, 6 - Atlanta, 7 - Chicago, 8 - St. Louis, 9 - Minneapolis, 10 - Kansas city, 11 - Dallas, 12 - San Francisco

name: FEDCHRTR
label: Federal charter flag
definition: A flag used to indicate whether the institution is chartered by an agent of the federal government.

name: FEDNAME
label: FEDNAME
definition: The name that identifies the Federal Reserve District where the institution is located.

name: FED_RSSD
label: Federal Reserve ID Number
definition: A unique number assigned by the Federal Reserve board as the entity's unique identifier

name: FI_EFFDATE
label: Last Structure Change Effective Date
definition: A date indicating when an institution's change/event is effective.

name: FI_UNINIM
label: FDIC's unique number
definition: FDIC's unique identifier number for holding companies, banks, branches and nondeposit subsidiaries. This value maps the branch back to the parent financial institution.

name: FI_UNINUM
label: FDIC UNINUM of the Owner Institution
definition: This is the FDIC UNINUM of the institution that owns the branch.  A UNINUM is a unique sequentially number added to the FDIC database for both banks and  branches.  There is no pattern imbedded within the number.  The FI_UNINUM is updated with every merger or purchase of branches to reflect the most current owner.

name: FORM31
label: FFIEC Call Report 31 Filer
definition: A flag (1=yes,0=no) that indicates whether and institution filed an FFIEC 031 Call Report. Commercial banks with domestic and foreign offices are required to file such a report.

name: FRM_CERT
label: Previous FDIC Certificate #
definition: A unique NUMBER assigned by the FDIC used to identify institutions and for the issuance of insurance certificates.

name: FRM_CHARTAGENT
label: Previous Chartering Agency
definition: Previous Institution Chartering Agency - State and Federal, Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: FRM_CLASS
label: Previous Bank Charter Class
definition: A code indicating the major groupings of institutions. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: FRM_CLCODE
label: Previous Numeric Code
definition: Numeric code which identifies the major and minor categories of an institution.

name: FRM_CNTYNAME
label: Previous County
definition: County where the institution is physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: FRM_CNTYNUM
label: Previous County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code.

name: FRM_INSTNAME
label: Previous Institution Name
definition: The legal name of the institution.

name: FRM_OFFNAME
label: Previous Branch Name
definition: Previous name of a branch office.

name: FRM_OFFNUM
label: Previous Branch Number
definition: An institution branch office's previous number.

name: FRM_OFF_CHARTAGENT
label: Previous Office Chartering Agency
definition: Previous Office Chartering Agency - State and Federal, Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: FRM_OFF_CLASS
label: Previous Office Class
definition: A code indicating the major groupings of institutions. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: FRM_OFF_CLCODE
label: Previous Office Numeric Code
definition: Numeric code which identifies the major and minor categories of an institution.

name: FRM_OFF_CNTYNAME
label: Previous Office County
definition: County where the branch was previously physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: FRM_OFF_CNTYNUM
label: Previous County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code where the branch was previously physically located.

name: FRM_OFF_NAME
label: Previous Office Name
definition: Name of the branch.

name: FRM_OFF_NUM
label: Previous Office Number
definition: The branch's corresponding office number.

name: FRM_OFF_PADDR
label: Previous Physical Street Address
definition: Street address at which the institution or one of its branches was previously physically located.

name: FRM_OFF_PADDR2
label: Previous Physical Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 at which the institution or one of its branches was previously physically located.

name: FRM_OFF_PCITY
label: Previous City
definition: City in which an institution's main office or one of its branches was previously physically located. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: FRM_OFF_PSTALP
label: Previous State Alpha Code
definition: State in which an institution's main office or one of its branches were previously physically located. The FDIC Act defines state as any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Virgin Island, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

name: FRM_OFF_PZIP5
label: Previous Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing the previous physical location of the institution or one of its branches.

name: FRM_OFF_PZIP5_RAW
label: Previous 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code of a branch office's previous physical location.

name: FRM_OFF_PZIPREST
label: Previous Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension of an institution or one of its branches previous physical location.

name: FRM_OFF_REGAGENT
label: Previous Regulator
definition: There are three federal regulators of banks and savings and loan institutions: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) - primary federal regulator responsible for state-chartered banks not members of the Federal Reserve System and state chartered savings banks; Federal Reserve Board (FRB) - primary federal regulator responsible for state-chartered commercial bank members of the Federal Reserve System.; Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) - primary federal regulator responsible for nationally chartered commercial banks and federally chartered savings and loan associations. Before 7/21/11, Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) - primary federal regulator responsible for federally chartered savings and loan associations, federal savings banks and state-chartered savings and loan associations. FDIC insured depository institutions are members of the Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF).

name: FRM_OFF_SERVTYPE
label: Previous Service Type
definition: Types of services offered by an office. Full service offices accept deposits, make loans, open/close accounts, have a loan officer on site, have normal hours, and a full time staff. Limited service offices offer some, but not all of these services. 11 = Full service brick and mortar office; 12 = Full service retail office; 13 = Full service cyber office; 21 = Limited service administrative office; 22 = Limited service military facility; 23 = Limited service drive through or detached facility; 24 = Limited service loan production office; 25 = Limited service consumer credit office; 26 = Limited service contractual office; 27 = Limited service messenger office; 28 = Limited service retail office; 29 = Limited service mobile office; 30 = Limited service trust office

name: FRM_OFF_SERVTYPE_DESC
label: Previous Service Type Description
definition: Service Type Description

name: FRM_OFF_STATE
label: Previous Office State
definition: State in which an institution or one of its branches was previously physically located.

name: FRM_OFF_TRUST
label: Previous Trust Power
definition: Previous trust power status of an institution or one of its branches.

name: FRM_PADDR
label: Previous Physical Street Address
definition: Street address at which the institution or one of its branches was previously physically located.

name: FRM_PADDR2

definition: Street address line 2 at which the institution or one of its branches was previously physically located.

name: FRM_PCITY
label: Previous Physical City
definition: City in which an institution's main office or one of its branches was previously physically located. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: FRM_PSTALP
label: Previous State Alpha Code
definition: State abbreviation in which an institution was previously physically located. The FDIC Act defines state as any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Virgin Island, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

name: FRM_PZIP5
label: Previous Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing the previous physical location of the institution or its branch office.

name: FRM_PZIP5_RAW
label: Previous 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code of an institution or one of its branches previous physical location.

name: FRM_PZIPREST
label: Previous Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension of an institution or one of its branches previous physical location.

name: FRM_REGAGENT
label: Previous Chartering Agency
definition: Institution's Previous Chartering Agency - State and Federal, Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: FRM_TRUST
label: Previous Trust Power
definition: Previous trust power status of an institution or one of its branches.

name: HCTMULT
label: HCTMULT
definition: The code that identifies the type of holding companyassociated with the branch.  The options are MULT, Multi-bank holding company; ONE, one bank holding company; and NONE, not a member of a bank holding company.

name: IBA
label: Insured offices of foreign banks
definition: Includes Bank Insurance Fund insured branches in the U.S. established by banks chartered and headquartered in foreign countries. These institutions are regulated by one of the three Federal commercial bank regulators and submit financial data to the Federal Reserve.

name: INACTIVE
label: Inactive
definition: Institutions that are currently closed but were once insured by the FDIC.

name: INSAGENT1
label: Insurance Fund Membership
definition: Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF), Bank Insurance Fund (BIF), Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: INSAGENT2
label: Secondary Insurance Fund
definition: As a result of the establishment of a single Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF) effective April 1, 2006, the Secondary Insurance fund is no longer applicable. Before April 1, 2006, Bank Insurance Fund (BIF) or Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: INSAGNT1
label: INSAGNT1
definition:
The primary insurer, insurance agent, or insurance status of an institution
BIF        = BANK INSURANCE FUND
DIF        = DEPOSIT INSURANCE FUND
SAIF      = SAVINGS INSURANCE FUND
STATE   = STATE INSURED
PRIV      = PRIVATE INSURED
SOVER  = FOREIGN INSURED
OTHER  = OTHER INSURED
NONE    = NOT INSURED
NCUA    = NATIONAL CREDIT UNION ASSOCIATION INSURANCE

name: INSAGNT2
label: Secondary Insurance Fund
definition: As a result of the establishment of a single Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF) effective April 1, 2006, the Secondary Insurance fund is no longer applicable. previously both bif and saif bank insurance fund - institutions that are members of the bank insurance fund savings association insurance fund - Institutions that are members of the Savings Association Insurance Fund

name: INSBIF
label: Bank Insurance Fund
definition: Institutions who are members of the Bank Insurance Fund. As of April 1, 2006 BIF was merged together with the Savings Institution Insurance Fund (SAIF) to create a single Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF). All FDIC insured BIF member institutions, that are still active or open, are now insured members of DIF.

name: INSBRDD
label: INSBRDD
definition: Demand deposits in insured branches located in Puerto Rico and U.S. Territories and Possessions.

name: INSBRTS
label: INSBRTS
definition: Time and savings deposits in insured branches located in Puerto Rico and U.S. Territories and Possessions.

name: INSCOML
label: Insured commercial banks
definition: Includes commercial banks insured by the FDIC. These institutions are regulated by one of the three Federal commercial bank regulators (FDIC, Federal Reserve Board, or Office of the Comptroller of the Currency). They submit financial reports to the Federal Reserve (state member banks) or the FDIC (state non-member banks and national banks).

name: INSDATE
label: Date of Deposit Insurance
definition: The date that an institution obtained federal deposit insurance.

name: INSDIF
label: Deposit Insurance Fund member
definition: A flag used to indicate whether an institution is insured under the Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF). As of April 1, 2006 the Bank Insurance Fund (BIF) was merged together with the Savings Institution Insurance Fund (SAIF) to create a single Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF). All FDIC insured BIF and SAIF member institutions that are still active or open are now insured members of DIF. 0 = No, not DIF insured and 1 = Yes, DIF insured. Note that institutions that became inactive prior to April 1006 will also have zero value.

name: INSDROPDATE
label: Date of Dropped Deposit Insurance
definition: The date that an institution relinquished federal deposit insurance.

name: INSDROPDATE_RAW
label: Date of Dropped Deposit Insurance
definition: The date that an institution relinquished federal deposit insurance.

name: INSFDIC
label: FDIC Insured
definition: Includes institutions insured by the FDIC.

name: INSSAIF
label: SAIF Insured
definition: Institutions who are members of the Savings Association Insurance Fund. As of April 1, 2006 SAIF was merged together with the Bank Insurance Fund (BIF) to create a single Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF). All FDIC insured SAIF member institutions, that are still active or open, are now insured members of DIF.

name: INSSAVE
label: Insured Savings Institution
definition: Includes savings institutions insured by the FDIC that operate under state or federal banking codes applicable to thrift institutions. These institutions are regulated by and submit financial reports to one of two Federal regulators (FDIC or Office of Thrift Supervision).

name: INSTAG
label: Agricultural lending institution indicator
definition: An indicator specifying whether an institution is primarily an agricultural lending institution.

name: INSTCRCD
label: Credit Card Institutions
definition: Institutions with total loans greater than 50% of total assets and credit card loans greater than 50% of total loans, including loans that have been securitized and sold.

name: INSTNAME
label: Institution Name
definition: The legal name of the institution.

name: INST_FIN_ACTV_FLG
label: Institution Status
definition: A flag to indicate whether a financial institution is active.

name: INSURED
label: INSURED
definition: Identifies commercial banks (CB),  savings institutions (SA), and insured branches of foreign banks (IB)

name: LATITUDE
label: Latitude
definition: The latitude of the physical address.

name: LAW_SASSER_FLG
label: Law Sasser Flag
definition: A flag, yes=1 and no=0 associated with OTS supervised savings associations that converted their charter to that of a commercial or savings bank. Converted associations remain members of the SAIF, but they become subject to supervision by one of the three federal banking agencies. Not Applicable as of March 31, 2006.

name: LOCN_PHY_ST_NUM
label: State Number
definition: A unique number identifying the state in which a physical address is located. This number is provided by the Federal Information Processing System (FIPS).

name: LONGITUDE
label: Longitude
definition: The longitude of the physical address.

name: MADDR
label: Mailing Street Address
definition: The street address portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MADDR2
label: Mailing Street Address Line 2
definition: The street address line 2 portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ADDRESS
label: Mailing Address
definition: The mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ADDRESS2
label: Mailing Address Line 2
definition: The mailing address line 2 of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_CITY
label: Mailing City
definition: The city portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_STALP
label: Mailing State Alpha Code
definition: The 2-digit state code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_STATE
label: Mailing State
definition: The state portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ZIP
label: Mailing Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ZIP5_RAW
label: Mailing 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ZIPREST
label: Mailing Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAINOFF
label: Main Office
definition: The main office for the institution.

name: MCITY
label: Mailing City
definition: The city portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MDI_STATUS_CODE
label: Minority Status Code
definition: A numeric flag used to indicate whether an institution is primarily a minority owned institution.

name: MDI_STATUS_DESC
label: Minority Status Description
definition: A descriptive flag used to indicate type of minority owned institution.

name: METROBR
label: METROBR
definition: An indicator for an area that contains a core urban area of 50,000 or more population

name: MICROBR
label: MICROBR
definition: An indicator for an area contains an urban core of at least 10,000 (but less than 50,000) population.

name: MSA
label: Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA)
definition: The Metropolitan Statistical Areas based on Census Bureau data, as defined by the US Office of Management (OMB) prior to the year 2000.

name: MSABR
label: MSABR
definition: The Metropolitan Statistical Areas (MSABR) are based on the 2020 Census. These areas correspond to the state/county/CBSA relationships as defined by the Census Bureau. For the purpose of the Summary of Deposits, MSAs reflect only the CBSA codes for metropolitan areas (use MSABR for the Summary of Deposits).

name: MSANAMB
label: MSANAMB
definition: The Metropolitan Statistical Area name in which the branch is physically located.  The Metropolitan Statistical Areas (MSABR) are based on the 2020 Census. These areas correspond to the state/county/CBSA relationships as defined by the Census Bureau. For the purpose of the Summary of Deposits, MSAs reflect only the CBSA codes for metropolitan areas (use MSABR for the Summary of Deposits). The SOD Deposit Market Share Reports display the last updated MSA Name (MSANAMB) associated with the MSA number (MSABR) in all prior years for time series analysis. The data download file contains the historic record of the MSA name based delineations of the statistical areas shown in prior years. Official Metropolitan Statistical Areas information can be found by visiting the website of United State Census Bureau (https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/metro-micro.html).

name: MSA_NO
label: Metropolitan Statistical Area Number
definition: The Metropolitan Statistical Area Number (MSA_NO) in which the institution is physically located. The Office of Management and Budget defines MSAs in terms of entire counties surrounding central cities, except in the six New England states where they are defined in terms of cities and towns within counties. Before 200 standards

name: MSTALP
label: Mailing State Alpha Code
definition: The 2-digit state code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MSTATE
label: Mailing State
definition: The state portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MUTUAL
label: Ownership Type
definition: Banking institutions fall into one of two ownership types, stock or non-stock. An institution which sells stock to raise capital is called a stock institution. It is owned by the shareholders who benefit from profits earned by the institution. A non-stock institution, or mutual institution, is owned and controlled solely by its depositors. A mutual does not issue capital stock.

name: MZIP5
label: Mailing Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MZIP5_RAW
label: Mailing 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MZIPREST
label: Mailing Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: NAME
label: Institution name
definition: The legal title or name of the institution.

name: NAMEBR
label: NAMEBR
definition: Branch name.

name: NAMEFULL
label: NAMEFULL
definition: Institution name.

name: NAMEHCR
label: Bank Holding Company (Regulatory Top Holder)
definition: Regulatory top holder is assigned by the Federal Reserve Board based on ownership and control percentages. Note: Information on bank holding companies is only as of quarter-end. Regulatory top holder is any company that directly or indirectly owns, controls or has power to vote 25 percent or more of a bank's or direct holding company's shares or controls in any manner the election of a majority of the directors or trustees of a bank or direct holding company or exercises a controlling influence over the management or policies of a bank or direct holding company. Information on Thrift Holding Companies that own Savings Associations but do not own banks is not currently available in the ID System. Source: Federal Reserve Board National Information Center data base.

name: NECNAMB
label: NECNAMB
definition: Name of the New England City Town Areas based on the PLACENUM.

name: NECTABR
label: NECTABR
definition: Code of the New England City Town Areas based on the PLACENUM.

name: NETINC
label: Net income
definition: Net interest income plus total noninterest income plus realized gains (losses) on securities and extraordinary items, less total noninterest expense, loan loss provisions and income taxes.

name: NETINCQ
label: Net income - quarterly
definition: Quarterly net interest income plus total noninterest income plus realized gains (losses) on securities and extraordinary items, less total noninterest expense, loan loss provisions and income taxes.

name: NEWCERT
label: New certificate number
definition: A new certificate number of an already existing FDIC-insured institution resulting from either a merger or an acquisition.

name: OAKAR
label: Oakar Institutions
definition: A member of one insurance fund that acquired deposits insured by the other fund, where that portion of the buyer's deposits remained insured by, and assessable by, the other fund.

name: OCCDIST
label: Office of the Comptroller
definition: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) District in which the institution is physically located. The six OCC Districts and their respective states are: Northeast - Connecticut, Delaware, District of Columbia, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, Rhode Island, Vermont, U.S. Virgin Islands Southeast - Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, West Virginia Central - Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin Midwest - Iowa, Kansas, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, South Dakota Southwest - Arkansas, Louisiana, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Texas West - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Colorado, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, States of Micronesia, Utah, Washington, Wyoming

name: OCCNAME
label: OCCNAME
definition: The name associated with the Comptroller of the Currency district in which the institution is located; 01-Northeast, 03 Central, 04 Western and 05 Southern.

name: OFFDOM
label: Number of Domestic Offices
definition: The number of domestic offices (including headquarters) operated by active institutions in the 50 states of the U.S.A.

name: OFFFOR
label: Number of Foreign Offices
definition: The number of foreign offices (outside the U.S.) operated by the institution.

name: OFFICES
label: Office
definition: A branch/office is any location, or facility, of a financial institution, including its main office, where deposit accounts are opened, deposits are accepted, checks paid, and loans granted. Some branches include, but are not limited to, brick and mortar locations, detached or attached drive-in facilities, seasonal offices, offices on military bases or government installations, paying/receiving stations or units, nondeposit offices, Internet and PhoneBanking locations where a customer can open accounts, make deposits and borrow money. A branch does not include Automated Teller Machines (ATM), Consumer Credit Offices, Contractual Offices, Customer Bank Communication Terminals (CBCT), Electronic Fund Transfer Units (EFTU), and Loan Production Offices Summary of Deposits information is required for each insured office located in any State, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico or any U.S. territory or possession such as Guam or the U.S. Virgin Islands, without regard to the location of the main office.

name: OFFNAME
label: Office Name
definition: Name of the branch.

name: OFFNUM
label: Branch Number
definition: An institution's branch office number used internally by FDIC.

name: OFFOA
label: Number of US Offices
definition: The number of offices operated by an FDIC-insured institution in all commonwealths and territories of the US, along with those in freely associated states under the Compact of Free Association

name: OFF_CNTYNAME
label: Office County Name
definition: County where a branch office is physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: OFF_CNTYNUM
label: Office County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code of a branch office.

name: OFF_EFFDATE
label: Office Structure Change Effective Date
definition: A date indicating when a branch office's change/event is effective.

name: OFF_NAME
label: Office Name
definition: The legal name of the office.

name: OFF_NUM
label: Branch Number
definition: The branch's corresponding office number.

name: OFF_PADDR
label: Office Physical Street Address
definition: Street address at which the institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: OFF_PADDR2
label: Office Physical Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 at which the institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: OFF_PCITY
label: Office Physical City
definition: City in which an institution's headquarters or one of its branches is physically located. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: OFF_PSTALP
label: Office Physical State Alpha Code
definition: 2-digit state alpha code in which the institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: OFF_PSTATE
label: Office Physical State
definition: State in which the institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: OFF_PZIP5
label: Office Physical Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing physical location of the institution or its branch office.

name: OFF_PZIPREST
label: Office Physical Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension of an institution or one of its branches.

name: OFF_SERVTYPE
label: Office Service Type
definition: Types of services offered by an office. Full service offices accept deposits, make loans, open/close accounts, have a loan officer on site, have normal hours, and a full time staff. Limited service offices offer some, but not all of these services. 11 = Full service brick and mortar office; 12 = Full service retail office; 13 = Full service cyber office; 21 = Limited service administrative office; 22 = Limited service military facility; 23 = Limited service drive through or detached facility; 24 = Limited service loan production office; 25 = Limited service consumer credit office; 26 = Limited service contractual office; 27 = Limited service messenger office; 28 = Limited service retail office; 29 = Limited service mobile office; 30 = Limited service trust office

name: OFF_SERVTYPE_DESC

definition: Service Type Description

name: ORG_ACQ_CERT_NUM
label: FDIC Certificate #
definition: The unique number assigned by the FDIC to a certificate of insurance issued to a financial institution.

name: ORG_EFF_NUM_DTE
label: Last Structure Change Effective Date Numerical Value
definition: A date indicating when an institution or branch office's change/event is effective.

name: ORG_END_NUM_DTE
label: End Date Numerical
definition: The date that ends or closes out the last structural event relating to an institution. For closed institutions, this date represents the day that the institution became inactive.

name: ORG_ROLE_CDE
label: Organization Role Code
definition: Codes include FI (Financial Institution), BR (Branch), and PA

name: OUT_CERT
label: Outgoing FDIC Certificate #
definition: A unique number of the outgoing institution assigned by the FDIC used to identify institutions and for the issuance of insurance certificates.

name: OUT_CHARTAGENT
label: Outgoing Chartering Agency
definition: The chartering agency of the outgoing institution - State and Federal, and the Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: OUT_CHARTER
label: Outgoing OCC Charter Number
definition: A unique number of the outgoing institution assigned by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) used to identify institutions that it has chartered and regulates (i.e. national banks).

name: OUT_CLASS
label: Outgoing Class
definition: A code of the outgoing institution indicating the its major grouping. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: OUT_CLCODE
label: Outgoing Class Code
definition: Numeric code which identifies the major and minor categories of an outgoing institution.

name: OUT_CNTYNAME
label: Outgoing County Name
definition: County where the outgoing institution is physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: OUT_CNTYNUM
label: Outgoing County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_FDICREGION
label: Outgoing FDIC Supervisory Region Number
definition: A two-digit number indicating the FDIC Supervisory Division or Region of the outgoing institution. 02 = New York; 05 = Atlanta; 09 = Chicago; 11 = Kansas City; 13 = Dallas; 14 = San Francisco; 16 = Office of Complex Financial Institutions (CFI)

name: OUT_FDICREGION_DESC
label: Outgoing Supervisory Region Description
definition: A description associated with the name of an FDIC supervisory region of the outgoing institution

name: OUT_INSAGENT1

name: OUT_INSAGENT2

The FDIC Act defines state as any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Virgin Island, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

name: SUBCHAPS
label: Subchapter S Corporations
definition: The Small Business Job Protection Act of 1996 changed the Internal Revenue Code to allow financial institutions to elect Subchapter S corporation status, beginning in 1997. Banks are required to indicate on the Call Report whether there is currently in effect an election to file under Subchapter S. Thrifts have a similar requirement as of March 1998. The most important IRS requirements to elect and maintain Subchapter S status are: There can be no more than 75 eligible shareholders and no more than one class of stock. (In general, shareholders can only be individuals, estates, and certain types of trusts. Certain retirement plans and charitable organizations will be eligible in 1998.) All shareholders must consent. Banks and thrifts converting to Subchapter S status must use the specific charge-off method for tax purposes rather than the reserve method of accounting for bad debts and recapture tax bad debt reserves over a period of six years, if the reserve method had been used prior to conversion. (Note: even though the specific charge-off method is required for tax purposes, an adequate allowance for loan and lease losses must still be maintained on the financial statements and Call Reports.) Banks and thrifts are subject to a built-in gains (BIG) tax, if the aggregate fair market value of assets is greater than their aggregate adjusted bases on the date of conversion to Subchapter S status. [Banks are required to indicate separately on the Call Report in December of each year, the deferred portion of income taxes reported in net income. For Subchapter S banks, some or all of their deferred tax assets and liabilities may be eliminated upon conversion to Subchapter S status; however, deferred taxes related to the BIG tax and the recapture of bad debt reserves must be recognized.]. A Subchapter S corporation is treated as a pass-through entity, similar to a partnership, for federal income tax purposes. It is generally not subject to any federal income taxes at the corporate level. Its taxable income flows through to its shareholders in proportion to their stock ownership, and the shareholders generally pay federal income taxes on their share of this taxable income. This can have the effect of reducing institutions' reported income tax expense and increasing their after-tax earnings.. The election of Subchapter S status may result in an increase in shareholders' personal tax liabilities. Therefore, S corporations typically increase the amount of earnings distributed as dividends to compensate for higher personal taxes.
